judgment.summary: A company with strong margins and cash flow, but facing near-term revenue and earnings headwinds and significant leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 15.4%; next earnings date is 2026-05-21.
positives: Operating margin 17.5% and Profit margin 10.3% indicate strong profitability on core operations.
Return on equity 19.6% shows effective use of shareholder capital.
Free cash flow $4.20B provides ample coverage for dividends and debt.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $665.10, suggesting upside potential.
Dividend yield 1.2% with a conservative payout ratio of 36.5%.
negatives: Revenue growth -11.1% YoY and quarterly earnings growth -9.8% indicate a challenging current environment.
Debt/Equity ratio of 376.02 is very high, suggesting significant financial risk.
Forward P/E 24.35 and PEG ratio 1.69 suggest the stock is priced for growth that may be difficult to achieve given current trends.

news.paragraph: Deere is set to report earnings on Thursday, with analysts expecting a decline in both revenue and earnings for the current quarter and fiscal year. Despite the anticipated downturn, the stock is trading at a forward P/E of 24.35, suggesting growth expectations remain for the longer term. Traders will be focused on management's outlook and any commentary regarding agricultural equipment demand.

headlines.title: Deere Earnings Due. It Also Has A Caterpillar-Like Growth Lever.
